summary: The branch stays within the task allowed paths and the ordinary test matrix passes, but the M4 save-format change is not an explicit nested schema. A review probe showed a save with an injected nested M4 postwarCandidate field can be re-checksummed, loaded successfully, and preserved in runtime.world.state.m4 while the state hash remains unchanged. The M5 worker-compatible hash evidence also uses a same-source direct adapter, which is insufficient for the task's Node/Worker-host contract requirement.

tests_run.result: ## feat/m5-sim-playable-loop...origin/feat/m5-sim-playable-loop; no dirty tracked or untracked files before writing this review handoff.

Diff is limited to allowed paths: apps/sim-runner, packages/protocol, packages/save-format, packages/sim-core, tests, project task/state/message files. No apps/web, desktop, client-core, ui, content-source, tools, gate docs, roadmap, manifest, release, or acceptance-matrix files are changed.
Hello, command/query, save/load, M4 replay, and M5 playable loop hashes matched. M5 Node and worker-compatible final hash bf042e42; M5 loaded hash bf042e42; postwar arrangements 1.
Architecture boundaries passed; determinism passed; root vitest 34 files/242 tests passed; web vitest 1 file/4 tests passed.
taskctl is not installed on PATH in this shell, so the repository script path was used for the required task validation.

Defect reproduced: after injecting state.m4.postwarCandidates[0].extraHiddenAuthority and recreating a valid checksum, loadSaveV1 returned loaded with stateHash bf042e42 and preserved the injected extraHiddenAuthority object.

decisions: REQUEST_CHANGES is required. The branch does not need an ADR or Human Gate for the intended M4 save-format schema evolution, but the current implementation is not acceptable because nested M4 save entries are not explicit DTOs and can preserve hidden authoritative fields.
Diff scope is acceptable. All changed files are inside M5-SIM-PLAYABLE-LOOP-001 allowed_paths, and no forbidden paths were touched.

The M5 loop correctly reuses accepted M4 replay, M3 postwar governance, versioned GameCommandV1 paths, bounded duplicate-settlement rejection, and deterministic state hashes. No manual node battle, full diplomacy, complex naval warfare, telemetry, server, multiplayer, arbitrary-code scripting, UI, desktop, or content-source scope was added.

The added sim.m3-postwar-governance-applied event-tail parser is directionally acceptable: it validates schemaVersion, kind, actor, IDs, method, reasonCodes, revisions, and rejects unsupported event kinds through the existing invalid-schema path.

risks.summary: SaveM4CampaignStateDto uses readonly unknown[] for all nested M4 collections. parseM4RecordArray/parseM4Record at packages/save-format/src/index.ts:1893 and copyJsonRecord/copyJsonValue at packages/save-format/src/index.ts:4619 preserve arbitrary nested keys. sim-core validateWorldStateV0 validates required fields but does not reject extra keys, so hidden data can survive load outside the state hash.

runM5PlayableLoopInWorkerCompatibleAdapter directly calls runM5PlayableLoopV1, and tests/sim-core-m5-playable-loop.test.ts:85 does the same. This proves same-function repeatability, not a worker/message boundary or Worker-host contract.

blockers.required_change: Replace the M4 unknown[] DTO with full explicit nested M4 save DTOs, parsers, and copy functions for campaignPlans, factionKnowledgeSnapshots, mobilizedForceCommitments, grainSupplyReservations, marches, fieldEngagements, sieges, withdrawals, warOutcomes, postwarCandidates, and their nested union/object fields. Do not preserve unknown extra keys from imported saves; either reject extra keys at the save-format boundary or copy only known schema fields before hydration.

Add negative save/load coverage proving malformed nested M4 fields and extra nested M4 keys are rejected or stripped before hydration, including the reviewed case: state.m4.postwarCandidates[0].extraHiddenAuthority must not load into runtime.world.state.m4 with an unchanged state hash.

Replace or supplement the same-source M5 worker-compatible adapter with a real worker-compatible contract path inside allowed files. The test must cross a serialization/message boundary comparable to the existing worker contract rather than directly calling runM5PlayableLoopV1 twice.

requested_action: Route back to the original simulation_engineer writer. Fix the M4 save-format nested DTO/schema boundary and the M5 worker-compatible contract evidence, then rerun the listed validation matrix plus the new malformed/extra M4 save schema tests and return an R2 writer handoff.
